reviewer.evidence: candidate.c is HEAD minus the two register-asm pins; no forbidden family constructs, named-intermediate t/mask is SOTN-accepted. WIP checkpoint at floor 9 honestly documented.

remaining_gap.summary: Identical to func_80061658: v0/v1 register-rotation with the mask's lui interleaved between the arg0[] loads. The post-jal schedule is BYTE-IDENTICAL to target except every v0/v1 occurrence is swapped (9 lines, 9 = masked-Levenshtein floor). GCC's local-alloc gives mask (longer live range, single pseudo across the loads) the lower-numbered v0; target gives mask v1.
remaining_gap.mechanism: GCC 2.7.2 local-alloc allocates the longer-lived `mask` pseudo to the preferred low reg ($v0). To match target, t's pseudo must get v0 instead. None of the pure-C levers tried (declaration order, block-local scope, mask placement, split-mask, dereference form) flip this preference.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: Two register pins (t->$2, mask->$3); no regfix rules.

next_hypotheses: Directed permuter from candidate.c with hint that biases the v0/v1 allocation between t (3-occurrence pseudo) and mask (single-occurrence-spanning-loads pseudo)
